restaurants.name: Café Clock
restaurants.signature_dish: camel burger with homemade spiced ketchup
restaurants.quote: A Fez icon, the original Café Clock rambles over several floors of a restored building at the upper reaches of Taa'la Kabira; the Clock offers a uniquely seasoned camel burger as the house specialty, and all the small bites and salads are delicious; confirmed by second reviewer as 'a charming café of higgledy-piggledy terraces' where the 'camel burger, dolloped with homemade spiced ketchup, is delicious.'

restaurants.name: Ishq Restaurant
restaurants.signature_dish: black couscous with caramelized pears and beef (EN); braised lamb shank with caramelised onions and steamed pear (FR)
restaurants.quote: Chic and elegant, Ishq is a contemporary gem at the Ain Zleten entrance to the Fez medina; the fine-dining menu includes black couscous with caramelized pears and beef, and poached fish with Sichuan peppers; the presentation of a warmed foie gras starter was remarkable. (EN) / The braised lamb shank with caramelised onions and steamed pear, followed by milk pastilla with ice cream and corne de gazelle, were exceptional; rooftop offers a breathtaking view over the Merinid Tombs and the medina. (Translated from French)
restaurants.merge_note: Both-pool merge: EN (Anita's Feast Feb 2025) + FR (MarocLovers Sep 2025). Opened October 2023 per MarocLovers.

restaurants.name: Bistro Laaroussa
restaurants.quote: The bistro serves home-style Moroccan specialties and Mediterranean dishes, everything prepared with fresh and local ingredients; the wine list is a delightful tour through Morocco's modern vineyards; we especially enjoyed pan-seared fish with vegetables and a glass of chardonnay from nearby Meknès.

restaurants.name: The Ruined Garden
restaurants.quote: The restaurant is set in the atmospheric ruins of a courtyard house a short walk from the medina's Tala'a Seghira shopping street; the chicken pastilla we were served was the best we've tasted in Fez: beautifully spiced and 'meaty', a nice dish to share; seven-hour lamb Mechwi also on menu.

restaurants.name: Restaurant Almandar
restaurants.quote: Friendly waiters welcome guests to this rooftop restaurant near Bab R'cif in the lower medina of Fez; the menu includes tagines, pastilla, couscous and Moroccan-style fast foods; complimentary starters of olives, zalouk and loubia or lentils begin the meal.

restaurants.name: Restaurant Gayza
name_variants: Restaurant Gayza Riad Fès Relais & Châteaux
restaurants.price_note: 600–800 MAD pp with drinks; 750 MAD discovery menu excl. drinks
restaurants.signature_dish: pastilla aux fruits de mer; tagine de fruits épicés; semoule à l'anis amuse-bouche
restaurants.quote: Translated from French: Chef Lalla Abida perpetuates a craft passed from mother to daughter across several generations, reinventing traditional Moroccan dishes with flair; we particularly enjoyed the semoule à l'anis amuse-bouche, the fine Moroccan salads with pumpkin compote and gnaoua broad beans, then the seafood pastilla and the spiced fruit tagine.
restaurants.flag_reason: Hotel restaurant (Riad Relais & Châteaux Fès / Riad Fès hotel). However: named chef Lalla Abida, 5 specific dish details, independent reviewer paid own bill.

restaurants.name: Café-restaurant Moulay Idriss
restaurants.signature_dish: couscous de bœuf; œufs au khliî
restaurants.quote: Translated from French: Siham and her husband Driss welcomed us with great warmth in this small room decorated with Moroccan salon seating, typical embroidery and woven carpets; they named the restaurant in honour of their son Moulay Idriss; the beef couscous and eggs with khliî (preserved meat confit) were freshly prepared traditional Fassi dishes.

restaurants.name: Restaurant Benyamna
restaurants.quote: Translated from French: Run by Abdel and his sisters Yasmina and Sabrina, with their mother cooking in the kitchen, this family home on Rue Blida near the Chouara Tannery welcomes you with simplicity and generosity; the menu highlights the classics of Fassi gastronomy, from slow-cooked tagine to seven-vegetable couscous.

restaurants.name: Restaurant La Morille Dar Diafa
restaurants.signature_dish: poulet fassi; tanjia (meat slow-cooked in elongated clay pot)
restaurants.quote: Translated from French: The cuisine reveals all the culinary finesse for which the people of Fes are renowned; we savoured an assortment of Moroccan salads including carrots, tchatchouka, broad bean salad and green beans, followed by the home-style Fassi chicken and the tanjia — meat cooked with spices in an elongated clay pot.

restaurants.name: Restaurant L'Amandier
restaurants.signature_dish: crème de lentilles vertes et escalope de foie gras poêlé; Rfissa au coquelet fermier
restaurants.quote: Translated from French: Chef Mohammed Achoual draws inspiration from the Fassi tradition — heir to Arab, Berber and Jewish cuisines — for seasonal creations: green lentil cream with pan-fried foie gras escalope, fine goat's cheese tart with aubergine elixir, and Rfissa made with farm cockerel in the pure Fassi tradition; the restaurant sources from local producers including Noor olive oil (among the world's best) and Domaine d'Aghbalou duck near Fes.
restaurants.flag_reason: Hotel restaurant within Palais Faraj Suites & Spa. However: named chef Mohammed Achoual, 5 specific dishes, named local producers — strong editorial detail.
